title: Dryopteris brasiliensis
intro: Dryopteris brasiliensis, also known as Brazilian male fern, is a species of fern native to the Americas. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Dryopteris brasiliensis include flavonoids, saponins, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, the plant is often used to make infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including respiratory problems and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Dryopteris brasiliensis is classified as a species of fern in the genus Dryopteris and the family Dryopteridaceae. It is a member of the order Polypodiales and the subclass Polypodiidae. The classification of D. brasiliensis is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Pteridophyta. This species is native to South America and is commonly found in shaded, humid areas. The classification of D. brasiliensis is a part of the larger system of plant taxonomy.
